title: Ascorbic-EDTA Buffer
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cjhjk","text":"For use in Wet-mount Method for Enumeration of Aquatic Viruses","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":11,"length":51}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"EC7F9109E845427CB3481FC391F76243","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/Wet-mount-Method-for-Enumeration-of-Aquatic-Viruse-c8pzvm"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"9flg7","text":"Note: An alternative Ascorbate-EDTA Buffer can be made with MgCl2 and Na2EDTA if Mg2EDTA is unavailable: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c1fsp","text":"- Combine equal parts of 0.125 M Tris-base, 0.1 M Na2-EDTA dihydrate, 0.2 M MgCl2 hexahydrate, and 0.2 M ascorbic acid, adjust with 10 N NaOH to reach a pH of 6-7.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dto3l","text":"    - 0.125 M Tris-base prepared by dissolving 0.151 g Tris-base in 10 mL ultrapure water ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3ls6","text":"    - 0.1 M Na2-EDTA dehydrate prepared by dissolving 0.372 g Na2- EDTA dehydrate in 10 mL ultrapure water ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9fjfv","text":"    - 0.2 M MgCl2 hexahydrate prepared by dissolving 0.407 g MgCl2 hexahydrate in 10 mL ultrapure water ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5fema","text":"    - 0.2 M ascorbic acid prepared by dissolving 0.352 g ascorbic acid in 10 mL ultrapure water","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Combine equal parts of 0.4 M Mg2EDTA and 0.8 M ascorbic acid, adjust with 10 N NaOH to reach a pH of 6-7.
Prepare fresh within 48 hours of use
creator: Verve Team
